chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI. Cr. Bail Appl No.1109 of 2020 Date Order with signature of Judge For hearing of bail application 20.08.2020 Mr. Nasrullah Malik, advocate for applicant Mr. Ali Haider Salim, DPG a/w Inspector/IO Mazhar Ali Jatoi, P.S. Surjani Town, Karachi ORDER Allegation against the applicant is that he along with co-accused named in FIR dispossessed the complainant from his Plot No.LT-1/137, Sector-08, Surjani Town, Karachi on 18.05.2020 and when the complainant went to claim its possession by showing his documents, the accused including applicant demanded ‘bhatta’ of Rs.500,000/- from him. 2. Learned defence counsel has argued that applicant is innocent and has been falsely implicated in this case; that FIR is delayed for 05 days; the investigation is over and applicant is no more required for further investigation. 3. Learned DPG and I.O. of the case have opposed grant of bail to the applicant. I.O. has informed that complainant is still in possession of the plot and except the demand of ‘bhatta’ no other evidence is available against the applicant. We, in the circumstances are of the view that case against the applicant requires further enquiry and he is entitled to the concession of bail. Accordingly, the application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.1,00,000/ (Rupees one lac) with P.R. bond in the like amount to be executed to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 4. The bail application is disposed of in the above terms; the findings made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial.

Accordingly, the application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.1,00,000/ (Rupees one lac) with P.R. bond in the like amount to be executed to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 4. The bail application is disposed of in the above terms; the findings made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. Judge Judge Rafiq P.A.
